- User wanted to run a scheduled Gmail triage job for Roy's inbox (roy@atpbos.com) to classify messages, archive junk/FYI, label action items, and draft replies (never send).
- Claude Code attempted to fetch new mail with `node scripts/gmail/gmail-fetch.cjs --full` to read the inbox.
- The fetch script exited immediately with error: `Missing GMAIL_CLIENT_ID in .env` — no OAuth credentials were provisioned on this install.
- Root cause: Roy's mailbox lives on the All The Power side; Gmail OAuth keys (`GMAIL_CLIENT_ID`, `GMAIL_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GMAIL_REFRESH_TOKEN`) were never set up in Spencer's Got Moles workspace `.env`.
- Impact: no mail was fetched, no labels applied, no drafts created, and the `.last-run.json` marker was not advanced — so the next scheduled run will retry the same window.
- Claude Code noted this is a known, recurring failure already recorded in memory and will repeat identically on every run until credentials land.
- Claude Code recommended two options: (a) add Roy's OAuth credentials to this install's `.env`, or (b) disable the job here and run it from whichever install owns the atpbos mailbox.
